content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
I Practices Commission
June 11,
1993
Sharon G.
Sanders
Asocate Counsel
Lo Angeles County Metropolitan
Transportation Authority
425
S.
Main St.,
6th Floor
Los Angeles,
CA
90013
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-93-190
Dear Ms.
Sanders:
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).’!
Your letter
states only a general question.
Therefore,
we consider it to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) •2/
QUESTION
You have asked for clarification of the phrase “local
gcrernment agency with jurisdiction in more than one county” as
used in Regulation 18750.1.
DISCUSSIN
Section 87300 requires every state and local government
acje”cy to adopt a conflict of inter’st code.
The code must
enumerate the positions within the agency which involve the making
participation in the making of decions which may foreseeably
have a material financial effect on any financial interest.
The
eoa also must specify those financi&l interests which must be
disclosed by employees holding those positions in order to avoid
conflicts of interest.
1!
-‘
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street. Suite 800
•
P.O. Bo 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95S04-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


Sñaron C.
Sanders
Our File No.
1-93-190
Page
2
Regulation 18750.1 establishes the procedures for
promulgating and adopting a conflict of interest code for local
government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county.
Section 82035 defines “jurisdiction” as:
.the state with respect to a state agency and,
with respect to a local government. agency,
gipj.
county, city, district or other
gs9raphica1 area in which it has jun
ction.
(Emphasis added.)
Funk and Wagnalls Standard College Dictionary
(1974)
defines
“jurisdiction” as the lawful “right to exercise official
authority, whether executive, legislative or judicial.”
Or,
in
th alternative the “territory within
-
. [which)
such lawful
authority may be lawfully exercised.”
You have not provided specific facts or asked specifically
about the status of the Los Angeles County Metropolitan
Transportation Authority.
If you desire more specific advice
about whether the Authority would be deemed to have jurisdiction
in more than one county, please provide more specific information.
Enclosed are the following previously issued Commission advice
lecters which may assist you:
1.
Woliver,
No. A-84-123--”In deimining whether an agency
principally located in one county has jurisdiction in another
county,
we look at whether the agency owns real property and to
what extent it has authority in the other county... .“
2.
Wellington,
No.
I-90—249--Th. lurisdiction of a city is
th: ntire city,
including an area that has been annexed to the
cit’.
3.
de Bortnowski,
No.
1—90—356—-The Inland Valley
Do- 1opment Agency’s jurisdiction is thD tcrritory within which
the agency may lawfully exercise its authority.
If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
